snps.effect_template: Variant in the dopamine transporter gene associated with altered dopamine reuptake and modest increase in ADHD susceptibility
Norepinephrine transporter variant associated with modest increase in ADHD risk and potential influence on stimulant treatment response
Alpha-2A adrenergic receptor variant linked to altered noradrenergic signaling and modest ADHD risk elevation
Serotonin transporter polymorphism with modest association to ADHD and potential modulation of emotional regulation

recommendation_template: ADHD has moderate heritability (~75%) with polygenic architecture. Genetic variants in neurotransmitter systems may influence treatment response. Consider comprehensive clinical evaluation for diagnosis. Environmental factors, behavioral interventions, and pharmacotherapy (stimulants, non-stimulants) should be personalized based on clinical presentation. Genetic testing may inform medication selection but is not routinely recommended for diagnosis.

studies.title: Integrative proteomics and pharmacogenomics analysis of methylphenidate treatment response
studies.finding: Identified protein biomarkers and genetic variants in dopaminergic and noradrenergic pathways associated with methylphenidate treatment response in ADHD

studies.title: Discovery of the first genome-wide significant risk loci for attention deficit/hyperactivity disorder
studies.finding: Identified 12 genome-wide significant loci for ADHD with heritability estimate of 22%, showing genetic correlation with educational attainment and other psychiatric disorders
